core_mechanism: self-organized criticality: a slowly driven many-body interacting system spontaneously evolving toward a marginally stable critical state that generates endogenous, scale-free bursts (avalanches/crashes) without external tuning or large exogenous shocks
brief_reason: The abstract names the 'self-organized criticality paradigm' — spontaneous evolution toward fragile, marginally stable states — as the recurring mechanism producing endogenous crises and crashes from out-of-equilibrium dynamics, alongside history-dependent multistability and non-convergent oscillatory/chaotic dynamics.
